title: lobelia arunachalensis
intro: Lobelia arunachalensis is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is a shrub-like plant native to the Himalayan region, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and stems. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, fever, and skin infections, and provide health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Lobelia arunachalensis, including infusions and decoctions, are used to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
